content: Dashboard view-model types live at src/dashboard/types.ts, intentionally separate from src/core/types.ts. The split: core/types.ts = durable domain projection of DB rows (Burrow/Run/RunEvent/Message); dashboard/types.ts = presentation projection for burrow watch and future burrow serve, with derived fields (activeRun), trimmed payloads (RunSummary omits prompt/metadata), and capped tails (eventTail, default 500). Keeping the projection in its own module avoids leaking presentation concerns into core types.

DashboardSnapshot follows the same additive-only versioning discipline as the §14.1 event envelope. Rules: (1) existing keys never change semantics or types; (2) new top-level + per-interface keys may be added, consumers MUST ignore unknown fields; (3) DASHBOARD_SNAPSHOT_VERSION (currently 1) bumps ONLY on breaking change — adding fields or enum members does not bump it; (4) optional fields stay optional forever; (5) enum members may be added, consumers MUST treat unknown state/kind/stream values as pass-through strings. The lock test (src/dashboard/types.test.ts) pins canonical key sets per interface so any rename/removal trips the test, forcing an intentional version bump.

name: wire-shape-key-set-lock-test
description: Lock test for stable wire shapes pins canonical key set per interface

content: Dashboard view-model types (BurrowCard.createdAt/updatedAt/destroyedAt, RunSummary.queuedAt/startedAt/completedAt, EventTailEntry.ts, DashboardSnapshot.ts) store timestamps as ISO-8601 strings, not Date objects. Reason: the envelope must round-trip through JSON without conversion (JSON.stringify(Date) emits a string but JSON.parse leaves it a string — using Date in the type would make the round-trip lossy). Mirrors the §14.1 event envelope (eventToEnvelope() converts Date to ISO string at the boundary). The conversion from row Date columns to ISO strings happens in the buildSnapshot builder (step 2).

name: id-based-tui-selection
description: ViewState (src/dashboard/tui-state.ts) tracks selectedBurrowId as an id (not index) so a snapshot that reorders burrows[] between ticks doesn't silently jump the cursor. Movement clamps via findIndex against the *current* snapshot; missing-id falls back to index 0. syncToSnapshot is a separate function (called per snapshot tick) that re-pins to the first remaining burrow when the selection disappears, drops mode back to list, and clamps detailScrollOffset to the new eventTail.length.

content: TUI reducer (src/dashboard/tui-state.ts reduce()) treats DashboardSnapshot as a *context* parameter, not state — pure (state, key, snapshot) -> state, never holds a reference past the call. No-op transitions (q after quit, j/k in detail mode, enter with null selection, esc in list mode, scroll in list mode) return the input state referentially-equal so downstream memoization is cheap. KeyName is a closed union; the switch is exhaustive (extending the union surfaces a missing-case error).

Detail-pane scroll offset (ViewState.detailScrollOffset) is 'lines back from the newest event' — offset 0 = live tail at bottom; pageUp INCREASES offset, pageDown DECREASES. Reducer clamps to [0, eventTail.length] using only the snapshot (termSize-unaware on purpose); the renderer applies a tighter viewport clamp once it knows actual terminal height. DETAIL_SCROLL_PAGE_SIZE is a fixed constant (10), not a function of termSize.

buildSnapshot(repos) is pure: same repos state ⇒ same DashboardSnapshot. The snapshot timestamp is the only impure surface — accept options.now (defaults to new Date()) so tests can pin it. Same pattern for runsLimit/eventTailCap defaults: pull from constants (DEFAULT_RUNS_PER_CARD, DEFAULT_EVENT_TAIL_CAP) but let callers override for tests and future stream-coalescing tuning.

BurrowCard.activeRun = first 'running' run, falling back to first 'queued' run, else null. Relies on RunsRepo.listByBurrow being newest-first (queuedAt desc); pickActiveRun does NOT re-sort. Callers MUST also see the active run inside BurrowCard.runs[] — activeRun is a convenience pointer for the renderer, not a separate fetch.

renderSnapshot returns exactly termSize.rows lines (joined by \n, no trailing newline), and every line is exactly termSize.columns characters wide (padded with spaces or truncated with U+2026 ellipsis). No ANSI escapes — color is deferred to the runtime so golden tests stay deterministic. Frame layout is fixed: row 0 title, row 1 ─, body, second-to-last ─, last footer.

List-mode column widths in src/dashboard/tui-render.ts: cursor=2, id=12, state=8, kind=7, active=20, last-event=cols-57. The active column must be ≥20 to hold 'running claude-code' (19 chars) without ellipsis — narrower values silently truncated agent ids during golden tests.

streamSnapshots wakes on EventBus.subscribeAll AND a setInterval polling fallback (default 1s). The bus alone isn't sufficient: burrow lifecycle (create/markDestroyed) and run lifecycle (enqueue/markRunning/finalize) don't publish to the bus today — they're plain SQL writes. Polling re-samples those changes; the bus is the fast path for events.append().

streamSnapshots coalescing uses a sticky pendingWake latch + trailing-edge sleep(coalesceMs) timer. Reset pendingWake=false right BEFORE buildSnapshot/yield, not after, so wakes that arrive during the synchronous build or async yield-suspend land in the next iteration's wait. Keeps a 100-event burst at ≤2 emissions while never losing the latest state.

TUI runtime (src/dashboard/tui.ts) is the only impure piece of the dashboard stack — owns alt-screen enter/exit (\x1b[?1049h/l), cursor hide/show (\x1b[?25l/h), raw-mode stdin, SIGWINCH listener, and the streamSnapshots consumer. Cleanup must be symmetric in a single finally block: detach 'data' listener, restore prior raw mode, dispose resize subscription, clear pending resize timer, write CURSOR_SHOW + ALT_SCREEN_EXIT.

TUI runtime merges external opts.signal and a 'q'/Ctrl+C keypress into one internal AbortController whose signal is handed to streamSnapshots — either path tears the stream down through the same finally block. quitReason='user' on 'q' or Ctrl+C; 'abort' on external signal; 'stream-ended' otherwise.

translateKeyBytes(buf): KeyName|null is pure. In raw mode the OS does NOT deliver SIGINT for Ctrl+C, so \x03 maps to 'q' inside the runtime. Recognized: q/j/k identity, \r/\n → enter, \x1b → esc, \x1b[A/B → up/down, \x1b[5~/6~ → pageUp/pageDown. Unknown chunks return null so paste / modifier prefixes are silently ignored.

TUI runtime injects stdin/stdout/onResize/initialTermSize for testability via TuiStdin/TuiStdout interfaces. process.stdin/stdout satisfy them; setRawMode/resume/pause are optional so PassThrough-style fakes work. Resize handler debounces SIGWINCH on the trailing edge (default 50ms): re-read stdout.columns/rows, render one frame. Tests pass a resize harness and synchronously call trigger() to drive the codepath without process signals.

name: watch-cli-two-faces
description: burrow watch CLI offers two faces over the same DashboardSnapshot view-model: TUI (default when stdout is a TTY) drives runTui from src/dashboard/tui.ts; --json (or non-TTY) writes streamSnapshots NDJSON to stdout. --once collapses NDJSON to a single snapshot for scripted readers. summary.mode/emitted/stoppedReason exposes the chosen face for tests.
